title: Money Fear
englishInput: Three months ago, after I left my fixed job to build my business, I started checking my balance repeatedly and delaying normal household spending. When income is lower or a large payment appears, the checking increases. I feel afraid, but on days when I review actual figures the tension decreases. I want to separate necessary investment from fear-driven delay.
chineseInput: 三个月前离开固定工作开始经营事业后，我开始反复查看余额，并拖延正常家庭支出。当收入较低或出现大额付款时，查看次数会增加；我感到害怕，但整理实际数字时紧张会降低。我希望区分必要投入与恐惧造成的拖延。
revisedEnglishInput: Three months ago, after leaving my fixed job, repeated balance checks increased specifically before business purchases and normal household spending.
revisedChineseInput: 三个月前离开固定工作后，反复查看余额的情况主要在购买事业工具和处理正常家庭支出前增加。
desiredDirection: Use actual financial evidence to distinguish necessary investment, normal spending, and purchases that can wait.
desiredDirectionZh: 根据实际财务证据区分必要投入、正常支出与可以延后的消费。
observedEvidence: Balance checks increased before large payments.
Tension decreased after reviewing actual figures.
unknownReality: Whether current business income can support the planned investment remains unverified.
